issue: truncate(text, length) is supposed to return the text unchanged whenever it already fits within `length` characters, cutting and adding the suffix only when the text is genuinely too long. Instead, a string whose length is exactly equal to `length` gets truncated: truncate("hello", 5) returns "he..." instead of "hello". The off-by-one is at the length comparison boundary. Strings clearly shorter or clearly longer than the limit are handled correctly.
